title: Desktop AI Test Agent Test Plan
description: A YAML plan of UI tests. One file may hold several tests under a suite. The agent reads the plain-language goal and drives the target app from the outside; YAML is the source of truth.

suite.description: Group these tests belong to (a bucket name). Often matches the file/folder, e.g. 'smoke', 'login', 'testzoo'.

tests.description: Map of test id -> test definition. The id is the key (e.g. DEMO-LOGIN-001).

testDefinition.description: One test. Only 'goal' is required; everything else refines targeting, verification, and traceability.

title.description: Short human-readable name shown in listings and the dashboard.

priority.description: Triage importance: P0 highest. Used for ordering/filtering only.

framework.description: Desktop UI toolkit of the target app.

target_window.description: Exact window title to attach to (FlaUI matches by Name), e.g. 'Sample Login App (.NET 8)'.

source_issue.description: Traceability: issue/work-item/ticket id or URL. Surfaces as a JUnit testcase property.

source_pr.description: Traceability: pull request that introduced or updated the test. Surfaces as a JUnit property.

authoring_agent.description: Who authored it: 'manual', 'claude-code', 'codex', 'copilot', or a team value.

risk.description: Business risk if this flow breaks. Informational.

ci_profile.description: Where it is expected to run, e.g. 'local-windows', 'github-windows', 'azure-windows'.

category.description: Test style. Scenario: a directed business flow (most tests). Smoke: a quick 'does it open / basic path works' check. Audit: inspect UI/accessibility metadata, no mutation. Monkey: exploratory/stress poking. Defaults to Scenario.

goal.description: REQUIRED. What the agent must accomplish, in plain language. Name the exact values to use, e.g. "Enter admin / password123, click Login, confirm success."

success_condition.description: Text the app shows when the goal is met (matched against a status label). Optional: omit and verify with an Assert action instead.

max_steps.description: Safety cap on agent iterations before the run fails. Typical 6-12 for a focused flow.

allowed_actions.description: Whitelist of UI actions the agent may use. An action outside this list is rejected, keeping runs bounded.

blocked_if.description: Human-readable preconditions that block the test (e.g. 'Target window not found').

existing_tests.description: Ids of existing automated tests this run complements (e.g. a TRX/JUnit testcase like 'MyApp.UiTests.LoginTests.HappyPath'). Surface as JUnit properties.
